name: Right Biceps Brachii
description: Two-headed muscle of the anterior arm, best known for flexing the elbow and supinating the forearm.
origin: Long head from the supraglenoid tubercle of the scapula; short head from the coracoid process.
insertion: Radial tuberosity and, via the bicipital aponeurosis, the deep fascia of the forearm.
action: Flexes the elbow and supinates the forearm; weak flexor of the shoulder.
clinical: Rupture of the long-head tendon produces a 'Popeye' deformity — a bulge in the lower arm.
